briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: In clear water, use dice/bug baits (Sexy Bug, Tumbleweed) on a Neko rig — incredible action and visibility. In stained water, stick to the traditional Neko worm for a subtler profile. Superglue the tungsten weights so they don't fly out when fish jump.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area w
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets y...
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

Yamamoto: The Chicken Rig is weedless unlike the Neko rig so it can be fished through brush, rocks, and docks. It has big bass appeal with the large Kut Tail profile. It's durable because the worm slides up the line on hookset

Palaniuk: 'My offshore rotation goes: 1) deep-diving crankbait to cover water fast and fire up the school, 2) swimbait for a slower moving presentation once crankbait bite dies, 3) big flutter spoon for vertical reaction bites on fish seen on elec...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
